channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Officials demand answers as crews work to restore power after another Puerto Rico blackout
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout that hit the entire island affected the main international airport, hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday announced the cancellation of more than $2.7 million in grants to Harvard University amid tensions between the school and the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, KRON4

item.title: New York family told missing woman was found dead, only to learn she's living in Detroit
item.description: A New York family is navigating an emotional journey after learning their sister, whom officials declared dead last year, is actually alive and living out of state.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Garcia, KRON4

item.title: Harris holds lead in hypothetical California governor race, poll shows
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris would enjoy a wide lead over the rest of the field if she were to enter the 2026 race for California governor, according to an Inside California Politics/Emerson College poll released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge will hear arguments as groups try to block Trump's executive order on elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday will hear arguments in three cases from national Democrats and voting rights groups that are challenging President Donald Trump’s recent executive order on elections, which, among other changes, would require proof of citizenship to register to vote in fed
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Frontier Airlines passenger describes terrifying landing after plane’s wheel breaks off
item.description: A Frontier Airlines flight lost a wheel during a terrifying landing at Luis Munoz Marin International Airport in Puerto Rico on Tuesday, a passenger said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE targets a community service program in its latest cost-cutting effort
item.description: A 30-year-old community service program for young adults that operates across the U.S. has become the latest target of the Trump administration’s campaign to slash government spending.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Arson attack probe at Pennsylvania governor's mansion looking into suspect's hatred of Josh Shapiro
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (AP) — Authorities investigating why a man set fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence are probing whether the suspect was motivated by the Democrat's Jewish faith or positions on Israel’s war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: IRS asked to revoke Harvard's tax-exempt status
item.description: Only the IRS can review and revoke the existing status of taxpaying entities and has traditionally done so without presidential interference, as mandated by tax laws.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, sheriff says
item.description: The dog, an Anatolian Pyrenees named Buford, regularly patrols the property, rancher Scottie Dunton said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Mississippi River named 'most endangered' by conservation group, cites FEMA cuts
item.description: The organization said the federal government plays a key role in protecting the river and helping homeowners prepare for, and rebuild after, major flooding.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration issues order to stop construction on New York offshore wind project
item.description: The Trump administration issued an order Wednesday to stop construction on a major offshore wind project to power more than 500,000 New York homes, the latest in a series of moves targeting the industry.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House invites Rachel Morin's mother to press briefing to share daughter's story
item.description: The mother of a Maryland woman who was killed by an individual who entered the country illegally addressed reporters at the White House on Wednesday as the Trump administration seeks to go on offense over the case of an individual who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-NYPD officer sentenced to 18 months after conviction for helping China stalk an expat
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former New York police sergeant was sentenced Wednesday to 18 months in prison in a U.S. case about China's pursuit of critics abroad, a sentence that came after two members of Congress urged the judge to spare him from time behind bars.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tariffs will cost families nearly $5k per year, Kentucky governor says
item.description: "That's months upon months upon months of groceries. That's months upon months of rent. That might be your entire annual deductible if you've got private health insurance coverage," he said.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Calalang, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michelle Trachtenberg's cause of death revealed
item.description: In February, police responded to a 911 call shortly after 8 a.m. at a 51-story luxury apartment tower in Manhattan’s Hell’s Kitchen neighborhood, where officers found Trachtenberg “unconscious and unresponsive,” according to an NYPD statement.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: MLB ump leaves game after foul ball hits him in the face
item.description: Veteran umpire Hunter Wendelstedt instantly hit the ground after he took a direct hit from the line smash off the bat of New York Mets center fielder Tyrone Taylor in the seventh inning.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, Arizona sheriff says
item.description: When an Arizona toddler wandered away from home Monday, a team of deputies, search and rescue volunteers and rangers began their search. In the end, it was a protective pup who track down the 2-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas DOGE calls for sanctions, audit of insurance company accused of spying
item.description: DOGE Lawmakers and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton have accused Superior of hiring private investigators to get information on lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, NewsNation

item.title: Colorado company to become first to refuel Space Force asset
item.description: Astroscale U.S. Inc., a Denver-based on-orbit services and logistics company, will perform two refueling operations of a U.S. Department of Defense satellite.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, NewsNation

item.title: Woman sues McAlister's Deli, claims tooth found in meal
item.description: A Myrtle Beach restaurant is being sued for negligence after a woman claims she cracked her molar on a tooth that was in her meal.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tatooine-like planet outside the solar system may orbit two failed stars, scientists say
item.description: A new Tatooine-like planet outside the solar system may orbit two failed stars, scientists reported Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zoom service restored after outage affects thousands
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: Vance to visit leaders of Italy, India
item.description: Vice President Vance will travel next week to Italy and India to meet with leaders there and visit cultural sites. Vance will be joined by second lady Usha Vance and their children for the trip, which will begin Friday with a trip to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: ACLU sues Defense Department school system over banning race- and gender-related books
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union has sued the Department of Defense’s school system for children of military families, asserting that the removal of race- and gender-related books and curricula violated students’ First Amendment protections against government censorship.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Slave descendants fight to protect their threatened island community at Georgia's highest court
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — Georgia's highest court waded Wednesday into a fight between Black landowners and local officials who have weakened long-standing protections for one of the South's last Gullah-Geechee communities founded by freed slaves.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with El Salvador's vice president in push for Abrego Garcia's release
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen traveled to El Salvador on Wednesday and met with the country’s vice president to push for the release of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a man who was sent there by the Trump administration in March despite an immigration court order preventing his deportation.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harvard's challenge to Trump administration could test limits of government power
item.description: On one side is Harvard, the nation’s oldest and wealthiest university, with a brand so powerful that its name is synonymous with prestige. On the other side is the Trump administration, determined to go further than any other White House to reshape American higher education.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Connecticut man who escaped house of horrors breaks silence after decades of stepmom’s alleged abuse
item.description: Kimberly Sullivan's stepson says he is feeling better and stronger nearly two months after escaping what police say is a case of extreme child abuse.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 men, 1 teen facing multiple charges in Louisiana for riding horses in Walmart
item.description: Three men and a teenage boy are facing charges after they rode horses through a Louisiana Walmart and posted a video of their adventure on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, WGN 9

item.title: Video shows ICE agents shatter car window during Massachusetts arrest: attorney
item.description: Federal immigration agents are accused of shattering a car window in Massachusetts while detaining a Guatemalan man on Monday in a caught-on-camera incident – despite his attorney saying they had the wrong person.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, Fox 5

item.title: Pixar Putt will be swinging into Riverton with family fun mini golf experience
item.description: Fans of "Toy Story", "The Incredibles", and more Pixar classics, get ready for a fun family activity coming to Riverton.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Why every MLB player will wear the same number Tuesday
item.description: Every MLB player, regardless of team or position, will be wearing the exact same jersey number on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: NYC helicopter tour company shuts down operations after crash that killed 6
item.description: The New York City tour company involved in a deadly helicopter crash is shutting down operations completely.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, Fox 5
